exact_data_needed: Any resistant or early-onset hypertension, hypokalemia, cramps or weakness, adrenal incidentaloma, OSA, or medications that could affect the aldosterone-renin ratio (ARR)?
Any muscle cramps, weakness, palpitations, constipation, paresthesias, or paralysis suggesting potassium, calcium, adrenal, or neuromuscular severity?
How much are you drinking and urinating, including polyuria, polydipsia, overnight urination, thirst intensity, access to water, and any dehydration or confusion?
Do you feel lightheaded when standing, faint, unusually weak, or better after fluids or salt?
Inspect and press lower leg edema
weight when dosing depends on it
Severe hypertension, hypokalemic paralysis, arrhythmia, stroke/ACS symptoms.
